observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive direct the:
(a) Municipal Treasurer and the Market Administrator to:
(i) to issue the necessary follow ups/demand letters to the accountable or liable stall owners/occupants for the settlement of delinquent accounts; (ii) initiate legal action against delinquent stall holders to enforce the collection of arrearages, as warranted; (iii) submit an updated master list of stall holders and reconcile the same with the actual occupants and numbers of market stalls to clearly establish that all units are properly accounted, recorded and included in the monthly report of market collections; and (b) Municipal Accountant to: (i) maintain the Special Accounts for market operations in the General Fund; and (ii) prepare the corresponding schedules to support the financial statements which will serve as tools in informing Management for evaluation and decision-making purposes.

We reiterated our recommendation and Management agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to: (a) adopt appropriate and legal remedies to enforce the settlement of cash advances granted to officials and employees, as applicable; (b) file a request for authority to write-off all dormant unliquidated cash advances pursuant to COA Circular No. 2023-008 dated August 17, 2023, if warranted; and (c) continue to strictly observe the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 on the granting, utilization, and liquidation of cash advances.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to (a) record appropriate amounts of impairment loss on overdue Accounts Receivable in accordance with paragraph 87 of IPSAS No. 41; (b) cause the immediate collection of the Accounts Receivable amounting to P40,000. 00 under the name of the establishment which the Municipality still engaged official transactions; (c) employ all appropriate remedies to recover overdue receivables, including issuance of demand letters and initiating legal proceedings, if necessary; and (d) request for an authority from COA to write-off dormant receivables following the requirements stated under COA Circular No. 2023-008 dated August 17, 2023.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the: (a) Municipal Accountant to set up the RPT/SET Receivables at the beginning of the year based on the Certified List of Taxpayers and Tax Due prepared by the Office of the Municipal Treasurer; and (b) Municipal Treasurer to improve the collection strategies on RPT/SET and initiate the available remedial measures in collecting the overdue taxes from delinquent taxpayers.

We recommended and Management agreed that the (a) Municipal Accountant prepare and maintain Supplies Ledger Cards (SLCs) for each commodity/stock to provide competent evidence to document the accuracy of the recorded cost of inventories; and (b) Inventory Committee to (i) undertake the complete physical count of all inventories of the Municipality; and (ii) prepare the corresponding Report on the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed period.

In relation to the above observations, we recommended and Management agreed to require the: (a) Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer as the Acting Property Officer to (i) conduct a complete physical count of PPE, prepare the corresponding RPCPPE, and submit a copy of said report to the Audit Team in accordance with Section 124 of COA Circular No. 2002-003 dated June 20, 2002; and (ii) reconcile the PPE records of the Accounting and Property Units at least on a quarterly basis; and (b) Disposal Committee to facilitate the disposal of unserviceable PPE in accordance with the prescribed guidelines and procedures.

We recommended and Management agreed to maintain its compliance with the guidelines outlined in Section 50 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I, to ensure the fair presentation of balances of the affected CIP and PPE accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended and Management agreed to: (a) verify, analyze and validate the existence of dormant accounts, and effect proper disposition of said accounts in accordance with prescribed procedures; and (b) leverage on the one-time cleansing of all dormant receivable accounts as provided by COA Circular No. 2023-008.

We recommended and Management agreed to: (a) continue to comply with the provisions of Section 287 of RA No. 7160, or the Local Government Code of 1991, as well as the guidelines set forth in DBM-DOF-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1 dated November 4, 2020, particularly on the appropriation and utilization of the 20% Development Fund; (b) strengthen the planning and monitoring mechanisms for the effective implementation of PPAs; (c) ensure that PPAs are identified through a well-considered and participatory planning process to minimize the need for reprogramming or discontinuance, and to avoid reliance on the “as the need arises” approach, which reflects gaps in planning; and (d) ensure the timely updating of the Comprehensive Development Plan in accordance with Item 3.2.1 of the aforementioned Circular to support strategic and evidence-based decision-making.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the (a) BPLO to: (i) strictly enforce the submission of Audited Financial Statements, or in their absence, a Sworn Statement of Gross Receipts/Sales or a copy of the ITR from taxpayers, to ensure the proper computation of any deficiency tax; (ii) exercise greater diligence in the implementation and enforcement of the provisions of the Municipal Revenue Code; (iii) ensure that the applicable tax rates are correctly applied based on the specific line of business declared by each taxpayer; (b) Municipal Treasurer to examine and verify the books of accounts and other pertinent records of any business establishments; and (c) BPLO and Municipal Treasurer to verify, assess, and validate the records of other businesses aside from the five sampled businesses and demand from the two business owners the payment of the computed under-collection of business taxes amounting to ₱52,463.87.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the (a) Budget Officer, Treasurer, and Accountant to (i) sustain and further enhance their efforts to maintain substantial compliance with the guidelines governing the appropriation, utilization, and accounting of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (LDRRMF), in accordance with the provisions of RA No. 10121, DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 dated March 25, 2013, and COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012; and (ii) address and complete the required documentation for identified deficiencies and submit the same to the Audit Team for verification; and (b) MDRRMO to ensure the optimal utilization of LDRRMF through a more realistic planning, strengthened implementation, and effective monitoring of all DRRM programs, projects and activities for the year.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Local School Board (a) officially transmit a copy of the SEF Budget to the Municipal Accountant, Local Budget Officer, and Municipal Treasurer within the prescribed timelines which is not later than seven days upon approval of the budget; (b) furnish the Sangguniang Bayan, DepEd Central Office, and DBM Regional Office 3 with copies of quarterly and annual utilization reports using SEF Budget Accountability Form No. 1; and (c) comply with the timely posting of the quarterly and annual utilization reports using SEF Budget Accountability Form No. 1 in the three (3) conspicuous public places identified by the Municipality for public disclosures.

In view of the above audit observations, we recommended and the Management, as well as the GFP agreed to continue implementing the proven and effective methods to sustain its pursuit of gender equality and increased awareness of gender issues and women empowerment.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Human Resources Management Officer (MHRMO) to (i) formulate a uniform policy on the hiring of JOs/COS and submit to the Management for review and approval, to ensure transparency and consistency on the recruitment process; (ii) prepare a duly approved and notarized contract for each JO employee upon hiring, clearly stipulating their specific duties and responsibilities, which can then be cross-referenced with their accomplishment reports, to comply with CSC Resolution No. 020790 dated June 5, 2002; and (iii) facilitate coordination with the Department Heads to ascertain the necessity of hiring JO personnel in each department, thereby maximizing the services of permanent employees.

We recommended and Management agreed that the: (a) Head of the BAC Secretariat furnish the Audit Team with certified copies of Contracts and Purchase Orders with the required supporting documents within five working days after issuance thereof; (b) Municipal Accountant (i) ensure the submission to the Audit Team of the paid DVs and supporting documents, Trial Balances, and Bank Reconciliation Statements, within the prescribed period; and (ii) ensure that certified copies of Contracts were duly received by COA before certifying the completeness of supporting documents and signing in box A of the Disbursement Vouchers; and (iii) Municipal Budget Officer promptly submit the monthly SAOBs to the Audit Team.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant (a) reconcile the remaining balance of ₱260,052.64 and remit to the BIR; (b) continue the practice of complying with the proper withholding of taxes; and (c) subsequently observe full and intact remittance of the total amount withheld to avoid or minimize the accumulation of unremitted balance in the custody of the agency, consistent with Section 114 (c) of the National Revenue Code as amended by RA No. 9337 and Revenue Regulation No. 2-98.

We recommended and Management agreed to continue to comply with the provisions of RA No. 8291 and its IRR, particularly with respect to the prompt remittance of premium contributions and loan amortizations to the GSIS.

We commended the Management for its strict compliance with the Home Development Mutual Fund Law of 2009 regarding the proper withholding of premiums and loan amortizations for its employees, as well as the prompt remittance of these amounts, together with the Municipality’s share, to the HDMF. We also recommend that said compliance be continuously practiced by the Municipality.

We recommended and Management agreed to continue to adhere with the National Health Insurance Act of 2013 (RA 10606) and its Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations to ensure the ongoing health insurance coverage of its employees.

We recommended and Management agreed to: (a) continuously observe the accounting and auditing rules and regulations, particularly in ensuring the regularity, validity, and completeness of transactions’ documents among others, to prevent the incurrence of similar deficiencies which resulted in the issuance of audit suspension; (b) cause the immediate submission of lacking documents identified in the remaining unsettled Notices of Suspensions issued by the Audit Team; and (c) demand the person/s named liable in the Notice of Disallowances for the immediate settlement of their respective accountabilities.

We recommended and Management agreed that the LCE instruct the:
(a) Municipal Treasurer to continuously employ effective collection strategies by updating and posting notices of delinquencies for property taxes at designated areas and conduct massive campaigns and public information dissemination on the value of paying taxes; and if warranted, enforce the provision of Section 254(b) of RA 7160 in the collection of delinquent taxes; and
(b) Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to reconcile their records of RPT/SET receivables in order to report reliably the said accounts in the financial statements.

In view of the aforementioned audit observations, we reiterated that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
(a) adopt appropriate and legal remedies to enforce the settlement of cash advances granted to officials and employees, as applicable; (b) file a request for authority to write-off all dormant unliquidated cash advances to the COA Audit Team Leader pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016, if warranted; and (c) continue to strictly observe the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 on the granting, utilization, and liquidation of cash advances.

We recommended and Management agreed that the LCE instruct the:
(a) Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer, as the Acting Property Officer to:
(i) maintain their respective PPE records; and
(ii) reconcile their records at least on a quarterly basis; (b) Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary journal entry to record the donated machineries; and (c) Disposal Committee to facilitate disposal of unserviceable PPE in accordance with the prescribed guidelines and procedures.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive require the (a) BPLO to:
(i) strictly enforce the submission of Audited FS or in its absence, the Sworn Statement of Gross Receipts/Sales or a copy of ITR from taxpayers for the proper computation of deficiency tax, if any; (ii) be more strict in the implementation and enforcement of the Municipal Revenue Code; (iii) ensure that the prescribed rates are correctly applied based on the line of business of the taxpayer; (b) Municipal Treasurer to examine and verify the books of accounts and other pertinent records of any business establishments; and (c) BPLO and Municipal Treasurer to verify, assess, and validate other businesses aside from the two noted businesses and demand the payment of computed under collection of business taxes.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
(a) maintain a repair and maintenance record/ history of each equipment/vehicle owned and/or operated by the Municipality as a control mechanism and monitoring tool to avoid unnecessary repair and maintenance expenses; and
(b) ensure that all disbursements are completely documented in compliance with Section 4(6) of P.D. No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

In view of the aforementioned audit observations, we recommended and Management agreed to: (a) continuously adhere with the guidelines set by Section 287 of R.A. No. 7160 or the Local Government Code of 1991 and DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1 dated November 4, 2020 on the appropriation and utilization of its Development Fund; (b) ensure proper planning and monitoring of the implementation of the PPAs; (c) ensure that PPAs are identified judiciously to avoid series of reprogramming and discontinuance of PPAs and refrain from adopting the “as the need arises” policy which indicates inadequate planning; (d) prepare a revised/updated PPMP/AIP, taking into consideration the duration and timing of the implementation of the newly identified PPAs, in cases where reprogramming is warranted; and (e) ensure that the Comprehensive Development Plan will be updated consistent with item 3.2.1 of the aforementioned Circular.

We recommended and Management agreed to: (a) continue its efforts to substantially comply with the guidelines set forth by R.A. No. 10121 on the appropriation, utilization and accounting of LDRRMF; (b) require the Municipal Budget Officer, Municipal Accountant and the MDRRM Officer to: (i) submit the necessary supporting documents for the six PPAs with total fund utilization of P648,030.00 but without corresponding appropriation; (ii) justify the use of P300,000.00 financial assistance for expenditure other than its intended purpose; and (iii) strictly monitor the disbursements of funds based on appropriation.

In view of the above audit observations, we recommended and Management agree to: (a) strictly adhere to the aforesaid provisions on the preparation and utilization of SEF Budget; (b) ensure that the disbursement vouchers are supported with complete documentation in compliance with Section 4 (6) of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012; (c) submit the lacking documents on disbursements totaling ₱2,114,313.93; (d) discontinue the allocation of funds for expenses that are not among those prioritized to be charged against the SEF; and (e) submit legal basis and/or justification on the aforesaid non-priority expenses.

We recommended and Management agreed to: (a) continue to comply with the allocation/attribution and utilization of the prescribed budget for GAD and the preparation of a relevant GAD Budget and Plan pursuant to the pertinent laws and regulations on GAD; (b) ensure the ongoing effective operation of the GFPS; (c) sustain a gender responsive monitoring and evaluation (M&E) system; and (d) continue the timely submission of the GAD Accomplishment Report to the Audit Team, in compliance with COA Circular No. 2014-001.

We recommended and Management agreed to: (a) continue to adhere with the provisions of RA No. 9003 or the SWM Act of 2000 on the appropriation and implementation of its SWM Program; (b) rigorously implement the Municipal Ordinances in relation to SWM and impose sanctions to violators, if warranted; and (c) take appropriate action to expedite the request for assistance from the donor agency on the use of donated machineries to make them operational.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
(a) instruct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to continue the practice of complying with the proper withholding of taxes and (b) direct the aforesaid officials to strictly observe the required full and intact remittance of all withheld amount to avoid or minimize the accumulation of unremitted balance in the custody of the agency, consistent with Section 114 (c) of the National Revenue Code as amended by RA No. 9337 and Revenue Regulation No. 2-98.

We recommended and Management agreed to continue to perform the agency’s mandate as withholding agent of the government with emphasis on strict observance of regulation on timely and intact remittances of employees’ and employers’ contributions in accordance with Section 6 (b) of Republic Act No. 8291 dated May 30, 1997.

We recommended and Management agreed to be more diligent in performing the agency’s mandate as withholding agent of the government with emphasis on strict observance of Pag-IBIG regulation on intact and timely remittances of employees’ and employers’ contributions in accordance with Section 3 of the Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act No. 9679 or the Home Development Mutual Fund Law of 2009, otherwise known as Pag-IBIG Fund.

We recommended and Management agreed to strictly observe the practice of intact and timely remittance of employees’ and employers’ contributions due to PhilHealth in accordance with Section 18 of the IRR of the National Health Insurance Act of 2013 and PhilHealth Circular No. 1, s. 2014

We recommended and Management agreed to: (a) continuously observe the accounting and auditing rules and regulations, particularly in ensuring the regularity, validity, completeness of transactions’ documents among others, to prevent the incurrence of similar deficiencies which resulted in the issuance of audit suspension; (b) cause the immediate submission of lacking documents identified in the remaining unsettled Notices of Suspensions issued by the Audit Team; and (c) demand the person/s named liable in the Notice of Disallowances for the immediate settlement of their respective accountabilities.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
(a) continuously adhere with the guidelines set by Section 287 of R.A. No. 7160 and DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1 dated November 4, 2020 on the appropriation and utilization of its Development Fund;
(b) ensure proper planning and monitoring of the implementation of the PPAs; and
(c) require the Bids and Awards Committee, BAC Secretariat, and Technical Working Group to ensure compliance with the pertinent provisions of R.A. No. 9184 and its IRR in all procurements through public bidding.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendations and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct:

The Municipal Treasurer to:
introduce collection strategy that would significantly help realize the collection of prior years’ RPT/SET receivables of the Municipality;

coordinate with the Accounting Office for the reconciliation of their respective records and to affect the necessary adjustments/corrections;

ensure that the list of taxpayers bear vital information, such as the exact addresses, to facilitate, if necessary, the sending of notices and other follow up communications relative to tax campaign activities and other collection strategy that may be implemented by Management; and

use available remedies, administrative and/ or judicial action, for the collection of taxes as provided in Section 256 of the Local Government Code of 1991.

The Municipal Accountant:
to coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer’s Office for the reconciliation of their respective records and to affect the necessary adjustments/ corrections in the reported balances of the affected accounts, if any; and

maintain subsidiary records to facilitate systematic recording and monitoring of individual accounts.

The Municipal Assessor to coordinate and timely provide the MTO with the needed assessment reports to facilitate the preparation of Certified List of Taxpayers by the latter within the prescribed period. Further, include in the programs/projects/activities (PPAs) the conduct of tax/land mapping to update the assessor’s records on real properties in order to come up with a more reliable basis in setting up of the subject receivable account.
observations.managementAction: Reiterated in Finding No. 1, Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation and the Local Chief Executive
direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to reconcile their records
of Real Property Tax (RPT) and SET receivables in order to report reliably the said
account in the financial statements. Moreover, we recommended that the LCE
instruct the Municipal Treasurer to improve collection strategies on RPT and initiate
the available remedial measures in collecting the overdue taxes from delinquent
taxpayers.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal
Accountant should (a) adopt appropriate and legal remedies to enforce the settlement
of cash advances granted to officials and employees, as applicable; (b) file a request
for authority to write off all dormant unliquidated cash advances to the COA Audit
Team Leader pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016, if
warranted; and (c) continue to strictly observe the provisions of Section 89 of PD 1445
and COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 on the granting, liquidation
and utilization of cash advances.

3.23 For the deficiencies discussed above, we reiterated and the Management
agreed to: (a) instruct the Municipal Accountant and Property Officer to: (i) complete
and reconcile their respective PPE records; (b) exert more efforts in locating
documents pertinent to the acquisition of its PPEs, maintain their respective PPE
records, and thereafter, conduct at least on a quarterly basis, the reconciliation of
their PPE information; (b) Disposal Committee to facilitate disposal of unserviceable
PPE in accordance with the prescribed guidelines and procedures; and (c) consider
the one-time cleaning of PPE account balances to improve the reliability of the PPE
accounts.

4.20 In view of the foregoing observations, we recommended and the Management
agreed to (i) collaborate efficiently with the Peace and Order Council; weigh the
sensitivity of the buy-bust activities where the establishment of Confidential Fund
may become a relevant consideration; and thereafter, follow the appurtenant
procedures prescribed under COA-DBM-DILG-GCG-DND JC No. 2015-01 dated
January 8, 2015; and (ii) ensure that the signatories of the DV are authorized in
compliance with Section 4 (5) of PD No. 1445.

5.8 We likewise recommended and the Management agreed to observe its
compliance on the aforementioned to instruct the Local Development Council to align
future development projects with the demands and within the parameters of “new
normal” era in consideration of the applicable regulations.

6.10. We recommended and the Management to: (a) appoint a Project Facilitator
who shall give assistance to Pakyaw Groups on the matter of group organization and
convention and preparation of contracts; (b) instruct the responsible officer to (i)
draw Pakyaw Labor within the vicinity of the project, or pakyaw labor groups that
belong to various Barangay Association contiguous to the project site; and (c) clearly
specify in the contracts the skills category of each pakyaw group and residence of its
members; and (d) require the BAC to strictly comply with the provisions of RIRR of
RA No. 9184 in the succeeding implementation of the projects through Pakyaw
Contracting System.

7.17 We recommended and the Management agreed that the Local Chief
Executive: (a) continue its efforts in observing compliance with the guidelines set
forth by R.A. No. 10121 and NDRRMC, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 2013-1;
and (b) require the MDRRM Officer and the Municipal Accountant to comply with
the planning and reporting for LDRRM activities as provided for under NDRRMC-
DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013 and COA Circular No. 2012-002.

8.13 We recommended and the Management agreed to: (a) continue to comply with
the RA 7910 and the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No.
2013-01 on the appropriation and utilization of GAD funds; (b) instruct the GFPS to
create a centralized GAD database that would consolidate information gathered and
compiled by all offices to include gender statistics and other relevant sex-
disaggregated data, as a basis in GAD planning, programming and policy making;
and (c) timely submit to the Audit Team the GAD Accomplishment Report in
compliance with COA Circular No. 2014-001.

9.25 We also recommended and the Management agreed to continue to intensify
the information campaign to the public to spread awareness of COVID-19 vaccine
benefits to achieve the Herd Immunity in the Municipality.

10.4 We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal
Treasurer and the Accountant continue to observe the withholding of taxes.
Accordingly, the aforesaid officials should be directed to strictly observe the required
full and intact remittance of all withheld amount to avoid or minimize the
accumulation of unremitted balance in the custody of the agency.

11.5 We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Treasurer
and the Municipal Accountant to continue performing the agency’s mandate as
withholding agent of the government with emphasis on strict observance of regulation
on timely and intact remittances of employees’ and employers’ contributions in
accordance with Section 6 (b) of Republic Act No. 8291 dated May 30, 1997.

12.5 We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Treasurer
and the Municipal Accountant to strictly observe the practice of intact and timely
remittances of employees’ and employers’ contributions due to PhilHealth in
accordance with Section 18 of the IRR of the National Health Insurance Act of 2013
and PhilHealth Circular No. 1, s. 2014.

13.4 We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Treasurer
and the Municipal Accountant should be more diligent in performing the agency’s
mandate as withholding agent and strictly observe the Pag-IBIG regulation on intact
remittances of employees’ and employers’ contributions in accordance with Section
3 of the Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act No. 9679 or the Home
Development Mutual Fund Law of 2009.

14.7 We recommended and the Management agreed to: (a) observe prudently the
accounting and auditing rules and regulations, particularly in ensuring the regularity,
validity, completeness of transactions’ documents among others, to prevent the
incurrence of similar deficiencies which resulted in the issuance of audit suspension;
(b) cause the immediate submission of lacking documents identified in the remaining
unsettled Notices of Suspensions issued by the Audit Team; and (c) demand the
person named liable in the Notice of Disallowances for the immediate settlement of
their respective accountabilities.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to direct: 

the Municipal Social Welfare Officer (MSWO) to be more diligent in the preparation of payrolls based on the approved list of qualified beneficiaries and implementation of financial assistance of same nature in the future to avoid incurrence of similar lapses;

the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant, in the implementation of similar forthcoming projects, to:

        (a) closely coordinate with the MSWDO in facilitating the review of payroll base on DSWD approved list of beneficiaries to prevent the inclusion of unqualified  recipients in the payroll; and 

        (b) consistently observe, monitor compliance on timely  submission of liquidation reports and refund, if any, of unutilized fund to the source agency to help maximize the timely and efficient utilization of limited government resources.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to:

instruct the designated officer responsible for the receipt, utilization, distribution and inventory of cash and in-kind donations to 

comply with Section II.1 (a) of the COA Circular No. 2020-09 by accounting all donations received during quarantine period; and

to submit all the required supporting documents of the donations received from all the donors.

We reiterated our recommendations and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the –

Property Officer to:

strictly comply with the required maintenance of complete and updated Property Card (PC) and Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE) for each class of property and to record the acquisition, adjustments and disposal thereof;

observe the annual submission of the actual physical inventory reports on PPEs to the Office of the Auditor not later than January 31 of each year pursuant to existing rules and regulations for timely review and validation.

Inventory Committee in coordination with the Property Officer, exert extra effort to complete the physical inventory of all PPEs of the Municipal Government and reconcile the report with the accounting records; and

Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Property Officer and Inventory Committee for the reconciliation of respective records and effect the necessary corrections/adjustments, if warranted, in the books of accounts.
observations.managementAction: The surge of Delta Variant of COVID-19 various hindered the management to fully implement the recommendations. Reiterated as finding No. 3 in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct:

the Municipal Treasurer to adopt more efficient procedures to ensure that all payments will be released immediately to the respective claimants in order to facilitate the prompt transmittal of transaction documents, such as cash/check disbursement vouchers, etc. together with complete supporting documents, to the Accounting Office for recording and timely submission to the Office of the COA Auditor for post-audit and evaluation;

the Municipal Accountant to ensure completeness of supporting documents before certifying the claim and allowing its payment. Further, strictly adhere with the provision of COA Circular No. 2009-006 on submission of reports, accounts and transaction documents within the prescribed period for post-audit to timely determine the regularity and validity of the transactions and correctness of the reported account balances; and

the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to facilitate the complete submission of DVs and supporting documents for December 2020 transactions. Likewise, cause the immediate transmittal of eight (8) paid DVs for the period March to October 2020.

observations.managementAction: Reiterated in Finding No. 2, Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant and the MSWDO to

exert extra effort to locate the covering MOAs, Contracts and other documents pertinent to the subject projects/programs in order to facilitate the preparation and issuance of necessary demand letters to the respective cooperatives, individuals accountable for the settlement of their loan obligations with the LGU;

send a separate invitation for a dialog to the respective representatives of the cooperatives/organizations to discuss the issues that hinders the settlement of their accountabilities in order to come up with a solution mutually acceptable to both parties which will lead to the possible settlement of the subject accounts; and
exhaust all remedies/actions for the recovery of the subject receivables and, if warranted, file appropriate request for write-off, if possible, of dormant accounts.
observations.managementAction: It was disclosed that a dialog with the representatives of the organizations were conducted during the year, however, the result was not favorable since some of the members informed the management that they settle the loans. As of date, the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer informed the audit team that a request for write off will be sent to the audit team.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer –
	to regularly ensure the deduction and withholding of applicable rate of taxes, for VAT and EWT, corresponding to the monthly payments to be made for electricity, water and communication expenses to be incurred by the municipal government in its operations in accordance with Sections 37(C) and 38 of Republic Act No. 10963 and Section 2 (J) of Revenue Regulations No. 11-2018; and

	to perform the agency’s mandated function as withholding/remitting agent of the national government by observing the monthly remittance of the VAT and EWT within the prescribed period to the Bureau of Internal Revenue (BIR) in support of the attainment of national government’s goal of promoting sustainable and economic growth for the country.

We recommended and management agreed to advise the Heads of MDRRMO, Accounting and the MDRRM Council to continue their strict observance of the guidelines set forth in NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-001. 

Further, remind the MDRRMO to always consider the prioritization of projects/programs/activities (PPAs) needing urgent response in the formulation of Annual Investment Plan (AIP) and utilization of the DRRM Fund to ensure that the funds will be used solely to its intended priority purposes and objectives.

We recommended and management agreed to advise the Municipal Treasurer and the Accountant to continue the practice of complying with the withholding of taxes. Accordingly, direct the aforesaid officials to strictly observe the required full and intact remittance of all withheld amount to avoid or minimize the accumulation of unremitted balance in the custody of the agency, consistent with Section 114 (c) of the National Revenue Code as amended by RA No. 9337 and Revenue Regulation No. 2-98.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to continue performing the agency’s mandate as withholding agent of the government with emphasis on strict observance of regulation on timely and intact remittances of employees’ and employers’ contributions in accordance with Section 6 (b) of Republic Act No. 8291 dated May 30, 1997.

We recommended and management agreed to direct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to strictly observe the practice of intact and timely remittances of employees’ and employers’ contributions due to PhilHealth in accordance with Section 18 of the IRR of the National Health Insurance Act of 2013 and PhilHealth Circular No. 1, s. 2014.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to be more diligent in performing the agency’s mandate as withholding agent of the government with emphasis on strict observance of Pag-IBIG regulation on intact remittances of employees’ and employers’ contributions in accordance with Section 3 of the Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act No. 9679 or the Home Development Mutual Fund Law of 2009.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer-

to maintain observance of the accounting and auditing rules and regulations, particularly in ensuring the regularity, validity, completeness of documentation of the transactions, among others, to prevent the incurrence of similar deficiencies which resulted in the issuance of audit suspension;

to cause the immediate submission of lacking documents identified in the remaining unsettled Notices of Suspensions issued by the Audit Team to facilitate timely review, evaluation and if found in order, the eventual issuance of the corresponding Notice of Settlement thereof; and

to prepare/issue demand letters to the person named liable in the Notice of Disallowances for the settlement of their respective accountabilities.

Further, in the event that subsequent issuance of audit suspension occurs, immediate compliance by management in the enforcement of settlement is advised to avoid maturing the same into disallowance.

2.13. We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the designated officer responsible for the receipt, utilization, distribution and inventory of cash and in-kind donations to (a) comply with Section II.1 (a) of the COA Circular No. 2020-09 by accounting all donations received during quarantine period; and (b) to submit all the required supporting documents of the donations received from all the donors.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to regularly reconcile the balances of the RPT/SET Receivables and Deferred RPT/SET Income with the Certified List of Real Property Tax Delinquency prepared by the Municipal Treasurer to ensure that the affected accounts in the financial reports of the Municipality are fairly presented.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the 

(a) Accounting Office to prepare and maintain complete PPE ledger cards and schedules for all PPEs to accurately present the balances of the accounts in the financial statements; 

(b) Property Officer to (i) maintain a complete and updated Property Cards; and (ii) conduct annual physical inventory count and prepare the corresponding report thereon; and 

(c) Accounting Office and Property Officer to regularly conduct reconciliation of their records/reports so that any discrepancy noted could be investigated and promptly adjusted in the books.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to 

(a) observe fully the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 on the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances; 

(b) require the accountable officers to liquidate overdue cash advances; and 

(c) file the request for authority to write off unliquidated cash advances to the COA Audit Team Leader and/or Supervising Auditor pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016, if all the conditions and requirements provided therein could be complied.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, the Vice Mayor and the Sanggunian concerned assert the right of ownership over the two (2) untitled lots of the market site and instruct the concerned Municipal Assessor to cause the submission of the TCT of the 130 square meter lot subject of the Deed of Exchange to establish ownership of the Municipality over said lots.  We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant reflect in the books the necessary information and correct carrying value of the Land account in compliance with Chapter 2, Section 2.1 of Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standard (PPSAS) which is now International Public Sector Accounting Standards (IPSAS).

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Municipal Development Council to appropriate the 20% Development Fund only to eligible projects authorized in DILG and DBM JMC No. 2017-001 dated February 22, 2017.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to 

(a) ensure the strict adherence to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-001 and Section 4 (6) of PD No. 1445 regarding complete documentation of claims against government funds; 

(b) direct the concerned Municipal Accountant to review the completeness and validity of the supporting documents before certifying the Disbursement Vouchers and allowing its payment; and 

(c) require the concerned Municipal Officials to submit the necessary supporting documents to prove the validity of the claim.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the 

(a) BPLO to (i) require the taxpayer to submit sworn statement on gross sales/receipts to be used in the initial computation of tax due; and (ii) require the submission of copy of Certified Income Tax Returns as required in the Revised Revenue Code of the Municipality; and 

(b) Municipal Treasurer to conduct examination of books, accounts and pertinent records of registered businesses operating within its jurisdiction to ensure that correct amount of business tax is collected.

We recommended that the incumbent Municipal Mayor and the Sanggunian   allot funds for the provision of municipal motor pool and assign permanent personnel in the maintenance of logbook for the utilization of the equipment to secure and safeguard the asset pursuant to Section 2 of PD No. 1445.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer agreed to strictly comply with the timely and intact remittances of taxes withheld in accordance with Section 114 (c) of the National Revenue Code as amended by RA No. 9337 and Revenue Regulation No. 2-98, thereby enabling the government to raise more revenues needed to finance government operations.

We recommended and the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant agreed to strictly comply with the timely and intact remittances of employees’ and employers’ contributions in accordance with Section 6 (b) of Republic Act No. 8291 dated May 30, 1997.

We recommended and the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant agreed to comply with the timely and intact remittances of employees’ and employers’ contributions due the PhilHealth in accordance with Section 18 of the IRR of the National Health Insurance Act of 2013 and PhilHealth Circular No. 1, s. 2014.
We recommended and the Management agreed to remit the Pag-IBIG premium contributions intact and on timely basis so that the employees can enjoy all the privileges of a Pag-IBIG member.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant notify the personnel concerned to settle the suspensions and disallowances thru submission of the required supporting documents and refunding of the disallowed amounts, respectively. The required supporting documents for the suspended transactions and the certified copy of Official Receipt for refund be likewise submitted to the Municipal Accountant for proper recording, and subsequently to the Auditor for adjustment of records.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to regularly reconcile the balances of the RPT/SET Receivables and Deferred RPT/SET Income with the Certified List of Real Property Tax Delinquency prepared by the Municipal Treasurer to ensure that the affected accounts in the financial reports of the Municipality are fairly presented.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the (a) Accounting Office to prepare and maintain complete PPE ledger cards and schedules for all PPEs to accurately present the balances of the accounts in the financial statements; (b) Property Officer to (i) maintain a complete and updated Property Cards; and (ii) conduct annual physical inventory count and prepare the corresponding report thereon;  and (c) Accounting Office and Property Officer to regularly conduct reconciliation of their records/reports so that any discrepancy noted could be investigated and promptly adjusted in the books.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to (a) observe fully the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 on the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances; (b) require the accountable officers to liquidate overdue cash advances; and (c) file the request for authority to write off unliquidated cash advances to the COA Audit Team Leader and/or Supervising Auditor pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016, if all the conditions and requirements provided therein could be complied.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, the Vice Mayor and the Sanggunian concerned assert the right of ownership over the two (2) untitled lots of the market site and instruct the concerned Municipal Assessor to cause the submission of the TCT of the 130 square meter lot subject of the Deed of Exchange to establish ownership of the Municipality over said lots.  We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant reflect in the books the necessary information and correct carrying value of the Land account in compliance with Chapter 2, Section 2.1 of Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standard (PPSAS) which is now International Public Sector Accounting Standards (IPSAS).

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Municipal Development Council to appropriate the 20% Development Fund only to eligible projects authorized in DILG and DBM JMC No. 2017-001 dated February 22, 2017.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the acting MENRO to (a) comply with the requirements of the law on waste segregation by collecting only those garbage that are properly segregated; (b) encourage active participation of barangay officials to support the solid waste management program of the government by educating their respective constituents on the proper segregation of reusable or recyclable materials from compostable wastes; (c) strictly enforce the use of Material Recovery Facility in barangay levels for final sorting, segregation, composting, and recycling so that only the residual wastes are transferred to Municipality’s station area; (d) ensure that only residual wastes are being collected by the MCWMC as stipulated in the MOA in order to avoid payment of costly hauling and tipping fee; and (c) provide a certain parcel of land appropriate for the implementation of its solid waste management activities, projects and programs.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to (a) ensure the strict adherence to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-001 and Section 4 (6) of PD No. 1445 regarding complete documentation of claims against government funds; (b) direct the concerned Municipal Accountant to review the completeness and validity of the supporting documents before certifying the Disbursement Vouchers and allowing its payment; and (c) require the concerned Municipal Officials to submit the necessary supporting documents to prove the validity of the claim.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Market Committee to (a) review and submit proposals on revision of market code to the Sangguniang Bayan which should include all the necessary provisions on the market operation; (b) prepare and submit to the Audit Team the monitoring report on the collection of fees from market stall rentals as of December 31, 2019; (c) strictly manage the public market operation particularly on the execution and implementation of contracts; and (d) monitor and intensify collections of market stall rentals.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the (a) BPLO to (i) require the taxpayer to submit sworn statement on gross sales/receipts to be used in the initial computation of tax due; and (ii) require the submission of copy of Certified Income Tax Returns as required in the Revised Revenue Code of the Municipality; and (b) Municipal Treasurer to conduct examination of books, accounts and pertinent records of registered businesses operating within its jurisdiction to ensure that correct amount of business tax is collected.

We recommended that the incumbent Municipal Mayor and the Sanggunian   allot funds for the provision of municipal motor pool and assign permanent personnel in the maintenance of logbook for the utilization of the equipment to secure and safeguard the asset pursuant to Section 2 of PD No. 1445.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer agreed to strictly comply with the timely and intact remittances of taxes withheld in accordance with Section 114 (c) of the National Revenue Code as amended by RA No. 9337 and Revenue Regulation No. 2-98, thereby enabling the government to raise more revenues needed to finance government operations.

We recommended and the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant agreed to strictly comply with the timely and intact remittances of employees’ and employers’ contributions in accordance with Section 6 (b) of Republic Act No. 8291 dated May 30, 1997.

We recommended and the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant agreed to comply with the timely and intact remittances of employees’ and employers’ contributions due the PhilHealth in accordance with Section 18 of the IRR of the National Health Insurance Act of 2013 and PhilHealth Circular No. 1, s. 2014.
We recommended and the Management agreed to remit the Pag-IBIG premium contributions intact and on timely basis so that the employees can enjoy all the privileges of a Pag-IBIG member.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant notify the personnel concerned to settle the suspensions and disallowances thru submission of the required supporting documents and refunding of the disallowed amounts, respectively. The required supporting documents for the suspended transactions and the certified copy of Official Receipt for refund be likewise submitted to the Municipal Accountant for proper recording, and subsequently to the Auditor for adjustment of records.

We recommended that the Vice Mayor stop the practice of granting Gasoline Allowance to the Sanggunian Officials and the Municipal Mayor to cause the refund of the same in the amount of ₱396,900.00 from the claimants.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to strictly adhere to Section 335 of RA No. 7160 by instructing the designated Municipal Official/s to 

(a) refrain from approving/issuing gas slips to personnel without assigned government vehicle; and 

(b) ensure that the gasoline/diesel/oil is issued only to government vehicles properly identified and supported by necessary supporting documents in accordance with COA Circulars No. 75-6 and 77-61.

We recommended that the Municipal Vice Mayor 

(a) strictly comply with the limitations of COA Circular No. 2000-05 and Section 55 (a) of the General Provisions of Appropriation Act for CY 2018 that transportation allowance shall not be granted when using government motor vehicle; and 

(b) refund the total amount of transportation allowance received which was also prohibited in COA Circular No. 2012-003 dated October 29, 2012.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to 
(a) immediately cause the issuance of the necessary demand letters to the person/s responsible for the liquidation of the ₱945,677.79 cash advances; and 

(b) cause the withholding of salaries of accountable officials for failure to submit necessary liquidation report and documents pertaining thereto.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, the Vice Mayor and the Sanggunian concerned assert the right of ownership over the two (2) untitled lots of the market site and 

(a) instruct the concerned Municipal Assessor to cause the submission of 
(i) original cadastre for the 12,000 square-meter lot covered by Tax Declaration No. 11-27011-00477; and 
(ii) TCT of the 130 square meter lot subject of the Deed of Exchange to establish ownership of the Municipality over said lots; and 

(b) allocate funds for the titling thereto. 

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant reflect the books the necessary information and correct carrying value of the Land account in compliance with Chapter 2, Section 2.1 of Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standard (PPSAS).

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to 

(a) ensure the strict adherence to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-001 and Section 4 (6) of PD No. 1445 regarding complete documents of claims against government funds; 

(b) direct the concerned Municipal accountant to review the completeness and validity of the supporting documents of the claim before certifying the Disbursement Vouchers and allowing its payment; and 

(c) require the concerned Municipal Officials to submit the necessary supporting documents to prove the validity of the claim.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant and the Acting Municipal Treasurer agreed to strictly comply with BIR Regulations and effect the timely and full remittance of the total taxes withheld every month, to help the national government in raising additional revenues.

We recommended and the Acting Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant agreed to continue the practice of remitting amounts due the Government Service Insurance System (GSIS) within the prescribed period in adherence to Section 6 (b) of Republic Act No.  8291.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to notify the personnel concerned to settle the suspensions and disallowances thru submission of the required supporting documents and refunding of the disallowed amounts, respectively. The required supporting documents for the suspended transactions and the certified copy of Official Receipt for refund be likewise submitted to the Municipal Accountant for proper recording, and subsequently to the Auditor for adjustment of records.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure the strict compliance to COA Circular No. 97-002 and instruct the Acting Municipal Accountant to send demand letters to officials and employees with outstanding cash advances to liquidate or settle the total amount of ₱351,122.79 immediately or within the specific time frame, otherwise, advise the Municipal Treasurer to withhold salaries of accountable officers who fail to liquidate their outstanding cash advances.
observations.reason: Non-compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002. Reiterated in Observation No. 4, Part II of this Report.

We also recommended that Acting Municipal Accountant reflect in the books the necessary information and correct carrying value of the Land account in compliance with Chapter 2, Section 2.1 of Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standard (PPSAS).

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor (a) require the Municipal Budget Officer to desist from charging claims/expenditures incurred in previous years but charged to current year appropriation; (b) cause the refund of the total amount of ₱1,013,929.90 representing unauthorized expenditure in CY 2018; (c) designate/assign only one municipal official who will approve and release gas slip request for control purposes; and (d) require the maintenance of logbook by the designated approving/releasing official for the recording of the gas slip approved and issued.

We recommended that the Vice Mayor stop the practice of granting Gasoline Allowance to the Sanggunian Officials and the Municipal Mayor to cause the refund of the same in the amount of ₱396,900.00 from the claimants.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to strictly adhere to Section 335 of RA No. 7160 by instructing the designated Municipal Official/s to (a) refrain from approving/issuing gas slips to personnel without assigned government vehicle; and (b) ensure that the gasoline/diesel/oil is issued only to government vehicles properly identified and supported by necessary supporting documents in accordance with COA Circulars No. 75-6 and 77-61.

We recommended that the Municipal Vice Mayor (a) strictly comply with the limitations of COA Circular No. 2000-05 and Section 55 (a) of the General Provisions of Appropriation Act for CY 2018 that transportation allowance shall not be granted when using government motor vehicle; and (b) refund the total amount of transportation allowance received which was also prohibited in COA Circular No. 2012-003 dated October 29, 2012.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to (a) immediately cause the issuance of the necessary demand letters to the person/s responsible for the liquidation of the ₱945,677.79 cash advances; and (b) cause the withholding of salaries of accountable officials for failure to submit necessary liquidation report and documents pertaining thereto.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, the Vice Mayor and the Sanggunian concerned assert the right of ownership over the two (2) untitled lots of the market site and (a) instruct the concerned Municipal Assessor to cause the submission of (i) original cadastre for the 12,000 square-meter lot covered by Tax Declaration No. 11-27011-00477 and (ii) TCT of the 130 square meter lot subject of the Deed of Exchange to establish ownership of the Municipality over said lots; and (b) allocate funds for the titling thereto. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant reflect in the books the necessary information and correct carrying value of the Land account in compliance with Chapter 2, Section 2.1 of Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standard (PPSAS).

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to (a) ensure the strict adherence to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-001 and Section 4 (6) of PD No. 1445 regarding complete documents of claims against government funds; (b) direct the concerned Municipal accountant to review the completeness and validity of the supporting documents of the claim before certifying the Disbursement Vouchers and allowing its payment; and (c) require the concerned Municipal Officials to submit the necessary supporting documents to prove the validity of the claim.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant and the Acting Municipal Treasurer agreed to strictly comply with BIR Regulations and effect the timely and full remittance of the total taxes withheld every month, to help the national government in raising additional revenues.

We recommended and the Acting Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant agreed to continue the practice of remitting amounts due the Government Service Insurance System (GSIS) within the prescribed period in adherence to Section 6 (b) of Republic Act No.  8291.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to notify the personnel concerned to settle the suspensions and disallowances thru submission of the required supporting documents and refunding of the disallowed amounts, respectively. The required supporting documents for the suspended transactions and the certified copy of Official Receipt for refund be likewise submitted to the Municipal Accountant for proper recording, and subsequently to the Auditor for adjustment of records.
